label: Mortgage By Profession
title: Profession-Specific Mortgages in Canada
metaTitle: Self-Employed, Medical & Athlete Mortgages — BC Broker
metaDescription: Specialized mortgage programs for self-employed, medical professionals, and sports professionals across Canada. Flexible income recognition and lender programs.
intro: Some incomes don't fit a T4 box. There are dedicated programs — and dedicated lender desks — for people who earn differently.
body:
Standard mortgage qualifying assumes you're a salaried employee with two recent paystubs and a stable T4 history. About a quarter of my clients aren't. They're founders running incorporated businesses, contractors invoicing through their own corps, physicians on residency contracts, surgeons earning T4A income from multiple hospitals, and professional athletes whose contracts span multiple jurisdictions.

Lenders have built specific programs for these income types — but most banks won't proactively tell you about them. The pages below outline the three biggest profession-specific programs I work with regularly.

highlights.title: Self-employed up to 90% LTV
highlights.description: Stated-income and BFS programs go up to 90% LTV with as little as 2 years of business history.

highlights.title: Physician programs
highlights.description: Several lenders waive student-debt counting and recognize 100% of contract income for residents and fellows.

highlights.title: Athlete income recognition
highlights.description: Specialty desks evaluate signing bonuses, multi-year contracts and offshore income for professional athletes.
